key_insight: Replayed-Prefix On-Policy Distillation (ReOPD) replaces expensive fresh student-environment rollouts with replayed teacher trajectories, while carefully choosing prefix positions to avoid unreliable teacher targets in multi-turn distillation.
engineering_relevance: It offers a practical way to train agentic LLMs for math reasoning and tool use with much lower interaction cost, zero tool calls during student training, and faster per-step updates.
method: Reuse pre-collected teacher trajectories as replayed prefixes
Let the student act at selected steps, while the teacher gives dense per-step supervision
Use a step-decaying prefix sampling schedule to emphasize early, more reliable histories
evidence_signal: Across mathematical reasoning with Python and search environments, ReOPD matched or improved OPD-level accuracy and was at least 4× faster per training step.
watch_item: The prefix-sampling policy must balance student relevance against teacher reliability; overly on-policy prefixes can create a prefix trap and degrade supervision quality.
paper_title: Multi-Turn On-Policy Distillation with Prefix Replay
